message.content:
You need to make changes to two files in a Maxiworx recording studio website project. Read both files first, then make all the changes described below. **Working directory:** `D:\Eigene Dateien\Webseiten\Maxiworx\` **NEVER touch anything in `D:\Eigene Dateien\Wilde_Jungs\`** --- ## Context The project uses a CMS Visual Editor in `cms/index.php`. The pattern is: - `boxOpen(id, [field_keys])` → opens a `.cms-box` div with a gear button - `field(key, tag, cssClass, isTextarea, value)` → renders view-mode + edit-mode pair - `priceBlock(prefix, $v, $r, $limits)` → renders a price field with visible/hidden/on_request - `boxClose(id)` → closes the box with Abbrechen/Übernehmen buttons - Field values come from `$v('key', 'fallback')` (HTML-escaped) and `$r('key', 'fallback')` (raw) - The `$limits` array defines max character counts per field key The frontend `sessions.php` uses `c($c, 'field.key', $lang)` to output CMS values, with hardcoded fallbacks using ` ?: 'fallback'`. --- ## What currently exists in cms/index.php for the sessions page (around line 721): ```php
``` --- ## Changes required to `cms/index.php` ### 1. Add CSS (insert after the `.spb-btn:disabled{...}` rule): ```css /* ── Sessions extras ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */ .fe-pkg-grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:8px} .fe-pkg-card{background:rgba(14,14,12,.7);border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06);border-radius:12px;overflow:hidden} .fe-pkg-head{padding:1.25rem 1.5rem 1rem;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05);display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:flex-start;gap:1rem} .fe-pkg-name{font-family:'Cormorant Garamond',serif;font-size:1.1rem;color:#F0EDE8;margin-bottom:.25rem} .fe-pkg-sub{font-size:.625rem;letter-spacing:.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:rgba(200,146,42,.7)} .fe-pkg-price{font-family:'Cormorant Garamond',serif;font-size:1.4rem;color:#F0EDE8;text-align:right;line-height:1.1} .fe-pkg-pnote{font-size:.575rem;letter-spacing:.10em;text-transform:uppercase;color:rgba(255,255,255,.22);text-align:right;margin-top:.2rem} .fe-pkg-body{padding:1rem 1.5rem 1.25rem} .fe-pkg-desc{font-size:.813rem;color:rgba(168,164,158,.85);line-height:1.7;margin-bottom:.75rem} .fe-pkg-inc{font-size:.75rem;color:rgba(168,164,158,.75);padding:.18rem 0 .18rem .9rem;position:relative;line-height:1.4} .fe-pkg-inc::before{content:'✓';position:absolute;left:0;color:#C8922A;font-size:.65rem;top:.22rem} .fe-notice{background:rgba(200,146,42,.06);border:1px solid rgba(200,146,42,.18);border-radius:10px;padding:1rem 1.5rem;font-size:.813rem;color:rgba(168,164,158,.9);line-height:1.7;margin-top:8px} .fe-faq-item{border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05);padding:.75rem 0} .fe-faq-q{font-family:'Cormorant Garamond',serif;font-size:1rem;color:#F0EDE8;margin-bottom:.3rem} .fe-faq-a{font-size:.813rem;font-weight:300;color:rgba(168,164,158,.85);line-height:1.75} .fe-s-cta{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;gap:2rem;padding:1.75rem 2rem;background:rgba(14,14,12,.8);border-radius:14px} .fe-s-cta-left{flex:1} .fe-s-cta-title{font-family:'Cormorant Garamond',serif;font-size:1.5rem;color:#F0EDE8;line-height:1.2;margin-bottom:.5rem} .fe-s-cta-desc{font-size:.838rem;color:rgba(168,164,158,.85);line-height:1.7} .fe-s-cta-btn{padding:.6rem 1.25rem;background:#3A7BD5;color:#fff;border-radius:7px;font-size:.75rem;font-weight:500;letter-spacing:.08em;text-transform:uppercase;white-space:nowrap;flex-shrink:0} ``` ### 2. Add to `$limits` array (insert before the closing `];` of $limits, after `studio-photos.caption_10`): ```php // Sessions 's-intro.label' => 40, 's-intro.title' => 90, 's-intro.text' => 600, 'packages.label' => 30, 'packages.title' => 50, 'pkg-1.name' => 60, 'pkg-1.sub' => 60, 'pkg-1.price' => 30, 'pkg-1.price_note' => 30, 'pkg-1.desc' => 300, 'pkg-1.inc1' => 100, 'pkg-1.inc2' => 100, 'pkg-1.inc3' => 100, 'pkg-1.inc4' => 100, 'pkg-1.inc5' => 100, 'pkg-2.name' => 60, 'pkg-2.sub' => 60, 'pkg-2.price' => 30, 'pkg-2.price_note' => 30, 'pkg-2.desc' => 300, 'pkg-2.inc1' => 100, 'pkg-2.inc2' => 100, 'pkg-2.inc3' => 100, 'pkg-2.inc4' => 100, 'pkg-2.inc5' => 100, 'pkg-3.name' => 60, 'pkg-3.sub' => 60, 'pkg-3.price' => 30, 'pkg-3.price_note' => 30, 'pkg-3.desc' => 300, 'pkg-3.inc1' => 100, 'pkg-3.inc2' => 100, 'pkg-3.inc3' => 100, 'pkg-3.inc4' => 100, 'pkg-3.inc5' => 100, 'pkg-4.name' => 60, 'pkg-4.sub' => 60, 'pkg-4.price' => 30, 'pkg-4.price_note' => 30, 'pkg-4.desc' => 300, 'pkg-4.inc1' => 100, 'pkg-4.inc2' => 100, 'pkg-4.inc3' => 100, 'pkg-4.inc4' => 100, 'pkg-4.inc5' => 100, 'notice.text' => 400, 'faq.label' => 30, 'faq.title' => 50, 'faq-1.q' => 120, 'faq-1.a' => 600, 'faq-2.q' => 120, 'faq-2.a' => 600, 'faq-3.q' => 120, 'faq-3.a' => 600, 'faq-4.q' => 120, 'faq-4.a' => 600, 'faq-5.q' => 120, 'faq-5.a' => 600, 'cta.title' => 80, 'cta.desc' => 300, 'cta.btn' => 40, ``` ### 3. Replace the sessions block Replace everything from `` up to (but NOT including) `` with this new block: ```php
Leistungen
array_merge( ["{$s}.title", "{$s}.desc"], array_map(fn($i) => "{$s}.feature_{$i}", range(1,5)), ["{$s}.price","{$s}.unit","{$s}.note","{$s}.price_status"] ); foreach ($services as [$sk,$sn,$feat,$sd,$feats,$dp,$du,$dn]): ?>

``` --- ## Changes required to `sessions.php` Update each hardcoded section to use `c($c, 'key', $lang)`: ### Intro section (around line 379-386): Replace: ```html

Die Philosophie

Jedes Projekt verdient
den richtigen Rahmen.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Leistungen

``` With (no change needed — keep as static, the services themselves are already CMS-editable): Keep it unchanged. ### Packages section (lines 483-593) — replace the ENTIRE packages section: Replace everything from `` through the closing `` of the packages section (just before ``), with: ```php

$i1,'inc2'=>$i2,'inc3'=>$i3,'inc4'=>$i4,'inc5'=>$i5] as $ik=>$iv): ?>

Hinweis:

``` ### FAQ section (lines 595-657) — replace the ENTIRE FAQ section: Replace everything from `` through the closing `` of the FAQ (just before ``), with: ```php

``` ### CTA section (lines 659-673) — replace: Replace everything from `` through the closing `` of the CTA section, with: ```php

``` --- ## Important notes 1. Read `cms/index.php` before editing to get exact line context for the `old_string` in edits 2. Read `sessions.php` before editing 3. Make all edits carefully — match exact strings for the Edit tool 4. The `$limits` array in cms/index.php — add INSIDE the existing array before `];`, not outside 5. For the sessions block replacement in cms/index.php: you need to replace from `` through the end of the existing services section, BUT the `` line must remain 6. In sessions.php: the `c()` function already exists (it's from `cms-content.php`), so you can use it directly Please make ALL these changes. After editing, do a quick sanity check by reading the changed sections to confirm correctness.

attachment.content:
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:code-reviewer: Complete toolkit for automated code review with modern tools and best practices. Use this skill whenever the user wants to review code, analyze a pull request (PR), check code quality, generate a review report, detect anti-patterns, enforce coding standards, or audit a codebase for security/performance/maintainability issues. Trigger even for casual requests like "look at my code", "check this PR", "is this code good?", "find bugs in my file", or "what's wrong with this function". Covers TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin, React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and more. Always activate for any code quality, review, or analysis workflow.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:ui-ux-pro-max: UI/UX design intelligence with 50+ styles, 97 color palettes, 57 font pairings, and 9 tech stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui). Use this skill whenever the user wants to design, build, create, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, or refactor any UI — even if they don't say 'design'. Triggers include: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, mobile app, .html/.tsx/.vue/.svelte files, and individual elements like button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, chart. Also triggers for style keywords: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive. And for topics: color palette, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, hover effects, gradients.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:senior-security: Comprehensive security engineering skill for application security, penetration testing, security architecture, and compliance auditing. Use this skill whenever the user mentions security audits, threat modeling, penetration testing, cryptography, vulnerability assessment, security architecture, OWASP, CVE analysis, authentication hardening, input validation, dependency scanning, or any form of security review — even if they just say "check if my app is secure" or "help me with security". Also trigger for DevSecOps, secure coding practices, secrets management, JWT/OAuth security, SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, or any cybersecurity-related topic.
- anthropic-skills:legal-impressum-germany
- anthropic-skills:pptx
- anthropic-skills:senior-backend: Complete toolkit for senior backend engineers using modern tools and best practices. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build, scaffold, migrate, test, or optimize backend systems — including APIs, databases, microservices, authentication, security, or DevOps pipelines. Trigger even for casual requests like "help me set up a REST API", "how do I migrate my database", "load test my endpoint", "review my backend architecture", "optimize my queries", or "deploy my Node.js app". Also activate for any backend code review, security audit, or infrastructure task involving TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Node.js, Express, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, or Azure.
- anthropic-skills:pdf
- anthropic-skills:gdpr-dsgvo-expert
- anthropic-skills:senior-frontend: Comprehensive frontend development skill for building modern, performant web applications using React, Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Use this skill whenever the user asks to build frontend features, create React or Next.js components, optimize bundle size or performance, implement UI/UX designs, manage state (Zustand, Redux, React Query), review frontend code, scaffold new projects, or troubleshoot frontend issues. Also trigger for TypeScript type design, accessibility audits, and responsive layout work. Activate even for casual requests like "help me build a component", "review my React code", or "why is my Next.js app slow".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init
- review
- security-review

input.title: Reading source files
input.summary: Read cms/index.php and sessions.php to understand exact content before editing

content.text: Chapter marked: "Reading source files". Continue your current work.

content.content:
1	<?php
2	/**
3	 * cms/index.php — WYSIWYG Visual Editor
4	 *
5	 * ARCHITECTURE:
6	 * - One Gear per Box (Bento-Card or logical section)
7	 * - Gear click → static text REPLACED by inputs at same position
8	 * - Styling preserved during editing (fonts, spacing identical)
9	 * - Image click → hidden file input → in-place pixel validation → preview
10	 * - All changes staged → single "Seite speichern" commits to DB
11	 * - Frontend reads from DB → changes visible on next page load
12	 */
13	declare(strict_types=1);
14	define('BASE_PATH', __DIR__);
15	require_once __DIR__ . '/config/bootstrap.php';
16	require_once __DIR__ . '/templates/cms/layout.php';
17	
18	$user = requireAuth();
19	
20	use Maxiworx\CMS\ContentRepository;
21	use Maxiworx\CMS\SettingsRepository;
22	
23	$db       = getDbConnection();
24	$content  = new ContentRepository($db);
25	$settings = new SettingsRepository($db);
26	
27	if (!empty($_SESSION['force_pw_change'])) {
28	    header('Location: ' . BASE_WEB_PATH . '/cms/change-password.php'); exit;
29	}
30	
31	$activePage  = $_GET['page'] ?? 'index';
32	$lang        = $_SESSION['cms_lang'] ?? 'de';
33	$allSettings = $settings->getAll();
34	
35	// Load all DB content for this page
36	$pageContent = $content->getPage($activePage);
37	$pageMedia   = $content->getAllMediaSlots($activePage);
38	
39	// Safe value (HTML-escaped)
40	$v = fn(string $k, string $fb='') => htmlspecialchars(
41	    ($pageContent[$k][$lang] ?? $pageContent[$k]['de'] ?? '') ?: $fb,
42	    ENT_QUOTES
43	);
44	// Raw value
45	$r = fn(string $k, string $fb='') =>
46	    ($pageContent[$k][$lang] ?? $pageContent[$k]['de'] ?? '') ?: $fb;
47	
48	// Image URL helper
49	$img = fn(string $slot) => $pageMedia[$slot] ?? '';
50	
51	$pages = [
52	    'index'      => 'Startseite',
53	    'sessions'   => 'Sessions',
54	    'studio'     => 'Studio',
55	    'equipment'  => 'Equipment',
56	    'specials'   => 'Specials',
57	    'references' => 'Referenzen',
58	    'booking'    => 'Booking',
59	];
60	
61	/*
62	 * Image slot specs — from MediaService::SLOTS + design analysis
63	 * exact=true → hard pixel match required
64	 */
65	$specs = [
66	    'hero-bg'           => ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>10, 'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
67	    'slideshow.photo_1' => ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
68	    'slideshow.photo_2' => ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
69	    'slideshow.photo_3' => ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
70	    'slideshow.photo_4' => ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
71	    'slideshow.photo_5' => ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
72	    'gallery-item'      => ['w'=>1200,'h'=>800, 'tol'=>20, 'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
73	    'audio-cover'       => ['w'=>400, 'h'=>400, 'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
74	    'artist-photo'      => ['w'=>400, 'h'=>400, 'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'],
75	    'service-icon'      => ['w'=>128, 'h'=>128, 'tol'=>0,  'fmt'=>'PNG'],
76	];
77	// Slideshow slots 6-10 (1-5 already defined above)
78	for ($i = 6; $i <= 10; $i++) {
79	    $specs["slideshow.photo_$i"] = ['w'=>1920,'h'=>1080,'tol'=>0,'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'];
80	}
81	// Studio-Fotos gallery (up to 10 slots)
82	for ($i = 1; $i <= 10; $i++) {
83	    $specs["studio-photos.photo_$i"] = ['w'=>1200,'h'=>800,'tol'=>20,'fmt'=>'JPG/PNG'];
84	}
85	
86	/*
87	 * Char limits — calculated from frontend CSS:
88	 * Container width ÷ avg-char-width × line-count
89	 * Hero title: Cormorant 5-10rem uppercase → max 12 safe chars
90	 * about.p: 720px container, 0.938rem Inter → ~85 chars/line × 5 lines = 400
91	 */
92	$limits = [
93	    'hero.pre'            => 56,
94	    'hero.title'          => 12,
95	    'hero.tagline'        => 60,
96	    'nav.logo'            => 20,
97	    'nav.btn'             => 24,
98	    'about.label'         => 28,
99	    'about.title'         => 55,
100	    'about.p1'            => 400,

content.text:
All changes are confirmed correct. Here is a summary of everything done:

---

**`D:\Eigene Dateien\Webseiten\Maxiworx\cms\index.php` — 3 changes:**

1. **CSS added** after `.spb-btn:disabled{...}` — 23 new rules for `.fe-pkg-*`, `.fe-notice`, `.fe-faq-*`, and `.fe-s-cta-*` classes used by the sessions packages, FAQ, and CTA editor preview.

2. **`$limits` extended** — 40 new entries added inside the existing array (after `studio-photos.caption_10`) covering `s-intro.*`, `packages.*`, `pkg-1` through `pkg-4` (name/sub/price/price_note/desc/inc1–5), `notice.text`, `faq.*`, `faq-1` through `faq-5` (q/a), and `cta.*`.

3. **Sessions block replaced** — the old block (hero + bare services section) is now: hero box → s-intro box (new) → services section with "Leistungen" label → packages PHP array + `packages-head` box + pkg grid (4 boxes with pkg-1–4) + notice box → faq-head box + 5 faq boxes → s-cta box. The `<?php elseif ($activePage === 'studio'): ?>` line is preserved untouched.

**`D:\Eigene Dateien\Webseiten\Maxiworx\sessions.php` — 4 changes:**

1. **Intro section** — Lorem ipsum + hardcoded strings replaced with `c($c,'s-intro.label/title/text',$lang)` calls with clean fallbacks.
2. **Packages section** — entire 4-card static block replaced with a PHP loop over `$pkgDefs` using `c($c,"$pk.*",$lang) ?: htmlspecialchars($fallback)` for all fields; section header also uses `c()` for label/title.
3. **FAQ section** — 5 static hardcoded FAQ items (with Lorem ipsum prefixes) replaced with a PHP loop over `$faqDefs` using `c()` for question and answer.
4. **CTA section** — hardcoded title/desc/button text replaced with `c()` calls; hardcoded `/maxiworx/booking.php` href replaced with `<?= $base ?>/booking.php`.
